title: Phallaria schimperi
taxonomy: Phallaria schimperi is classified as a species within the family Commelinaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as spiderworts. This classification places Phallaria schimperi within the order Commelinales and the clade monocots. Further classification includes the kingdom Plantae and the clade angiosperms. Phallaria schimperi is a genus of flowering plants that is native to East Africa. The Commelinaceae family, which includes Phallaria schimperi, is characterized by its unique flowers and adaptations to hot and dry environments.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties.
